item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So AWPI was a British WWS before WWS was a thing. I smell a tale…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I almost immediately thought "squirrel chestburster" when reading this. Except for the fact that it comes out of your mouth.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I would love The Atypical Woodland Preservation Institute to be a thing hope you come up with more and other members use the idea
Ps this is cool

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, as specific as it is I didn't think it'd be a problem, just wasn't sure if it might be down the road or if it might be reason to elaborate and distinguish this one more, though it kind of works best for how simple, sweet and brief it is.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I wasn't aware of the cartoon programme before writing/posting (bit old for all that bollocks now) but I realise they are fairly similar. Its quite a big coincidence - squirrels that inhabit people's stomachs isn't exactly a common trope.

I reckon they're different enough for this to stay up, but if a mod or anyone important would like to say anything official on the matter it'd be welcome.

EDIT: Had a brief chat to Bright on #site17 about it and he says it should be okay to stay as is.

+ Chat Log
- Close

<MouseMats>: BogLeech left a comment on the SCP itself.
<MouseMats>: It is a squirrel monster from a ninja turtles animation.
<MouseMats>: Both the SCP and the creature are squirrels which forcefully inhabit the stomachs of humans.
<MouseMats>: I acknowledge that its a big coincidence, but I honestly had no idea 'squirreloids' were a thing till Bogleach mentioend it.
<Bright>: MEh. It's not a big deal, these things happen.
<Bright>: See: 173/weeping angels
<Bright>: See: Sleepy Hollow/Bone Flute
<MouseMats>: 173 was pre-angels, though. I'm pretty sure TMNT predates 2327, so I was just asking for clarification on the matter.
<MouseMats>: Cheers though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I know this is accidental and it may not matter, but Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles - the current series, airing on Nickelodeon - beat you to this concept by a few months; mutated squirrels that look normal, but gestate parasitically in stomachs before climbing their way up through the throat and getting vomited out.

Could use some adjustments or some strange additional twists and details to differentiate it, if it matters.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not to begin with, but when I saw that was what it was called I was like 'Oh, I definitely need to have this in there somewhere!'

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You wrote this just so you could use esophagogastroduodenoscopy in a sentence, didn't you?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thank you.

I feex redundant words of redundancy.

I think the best way to pull off body horror is to be really technical about it, but I appreciate the criticism. Some medical words are stupid long.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hmm. It wasn't really my intent to make them 'a thing' unless I come up with other woodland animals that could have appropriate links. A small group of zoologists and ecologists in England trying to ensure weird animals they find don't go extinct has somewhat limited scope, but if someone takes AWPI and does something interesting with them I won't complain.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
D-class personnel are to be purposefully introduced to SCP-2327 for this purpose

This sounds a bit redundant…
Other than a couple tone-breaks, and (in my oppinion) over-complicating the descriptions with technical words when some simpler ones could work, the idea itself is pretty solid. +1 anyway.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Agreed, +1

My first though was "Oh God, not more squirrels," but non-violently absorbing a GOI is neat.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The GOI here is what earned the +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The Atypical Woodland Preservation Institute

Are you trying to make a GOI? I think you're trying to make a GOI.

I approve. ( ͡° ͜ʖ ͡°)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the image of vomiting squirrels. Article could use a little structural work, though. The paragraph about them having genders but no genitalia could stand to be placed elsewhere, and possibly whittled down.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1

I have a strange, strange feeling that these aren't really anomalous. Hear me out. Yes, they reproduce in a way similar to an adorable & fuzzy xenomorph…but that seems to be natural for them. At least, that's how I see it. They're strange, yes, but there are *far* stranger things in the animal kingdom. And that's part of why I'm +1ing this. It reminds me a little of the alien we have. A completely non-anomalous life-form that is really only being kept by the Foundation because while it's completely natural…it's just weird.

Though, while I might've missed it, do they have to use humans to reproduce? Or can they use other animals?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feel like not naming the zoologist B. Potter was a missed opportunity.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks!

1 and 4 were suggestions I agreed with with a certain level of hesitation. If others agree I'll remove them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So, it's a squirrel what reproduces parasitically.

I don't know. Something about this article failed to garner too much interest from me, and the tone broke in places. Neutral vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You know, an uncontained population of these could be a real problem. Like, they're basically a contagious disease. Low fatality rate and mild symptoms, sure, but having an outbreak of parasitoid squirrels could cause a community all kinds of trouble.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I enjoyed this, although I feel like footnotes 1 and 4 possibly break the tone of the article slightly.

That could just be me though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is my first SCP that I've posted to the mainlist, so hopefully it'll be up to snuff.

I'd like to thank Soulless, TwistedGears, DrippingPitch, Adelia, Ganelon and Cimmerian for having a look at it in the chat, and for Eskobar's critique on the forums. I'm sorry if I've forgotten anyone.
